description: Annotation layer that explains *what kind of proof problem* each Verity task is and *what proof skills* it exercises. It complements the machine-readable per-task metadata (proof_family, property_class, difficulty) in benchmark-versions/<v>.json with reviewer-curated skill tags and an empirically-derived snapshot of how models fail.

decoupling.statement: This file is NOT an input to result_key and never triggers a rerun. It is a separate, hand-maintainable layer keyed by task_ref. Each label records the task_fingerprint it was reviewed against so staleness can be detected (label fingerprint != current version fingerprint) without invalidating any stored result.
decoupling.staleness_check: A label is 'current' for a version when its task_fingerprint matches that version's task_fingerprint for the same task_ref. A drifted fingerprint flags the label for re-review; it does not block result reuse.

proof_family.description: Coarse proof archetype (functional_correctness, state_preservation_local_effects, authorization_enablement, protocol_transition_correctness, refinement_equivalence).

property_class.description: Finer-grained property being proven (e.g. accounting_conservation, access_control_identity, linked_list_acyclicity).

difficulty.description: Author-assigned a-priori difficulty. Expected vocabulary: easy | medium | hard. NOTE: v0.1 contains one outlier label 'low' (ipor/plasma_vault_redeem_split/fee_payout_bounded_by_fee_free); treat as 'easy' until the version metadata is corrected.

skills.description: Reviewer-curated proof-skill tags from skill_vocabulary below. Captures the reasoning a passing proof requires, independent of any model's outcome.

observed_failure_modes.description: Snapshot of parsed Lean failure modes (see analysis/failure_modes.json) over enriched failing attempts, reproducible via scripts/extract_task_features.py. Informational; refreshes as more models are run.

empirical_hardness.description: 1 - cohort_pass_rate over full-coverage models (see analysis/task_features.json). Distinct from the author-assigned 'difficulty'.

skill_vocabulary.description: Integer/modular arithmetic, bounds, rounding, overflow, fixed-point math.
Sums/balances/totals preserved across an operation (accounting invariants).
Branching on conditionals/enums; discharging each control-flow path (split_ifs).
Unfolding contract semantics, the Verity state monad, or spec definitions to a tractable goal.
Reasoning about storage reads/writes threaded through sequential state transitions.
Showing a property holds after a transition given it held before.
Caller identity, authorization gates, permitted-vs-rejected execution.
Equational rewriting and simp-set orchestration to normalize goals.
Reasoning under universal/existential quantifiers over sets, maps, or indices.
Finite sets, linked lists, trees, arrays (membership, bijection, acyclicity).
Showing an implementation refines/equals an abstract spec model.
Reasoning about revert/exceptional paths and that disallowed actions fail.

label_provenance.skills: reviewer-curated (Th0rgal) from task proof_family/property_class and a reading of the target theorem
label_provenance.observed_failure_modes: auto-derived from v0.1 detailed artifacts for the full-coverage strong models (zai/glm-5.2, minimax/minimax-m3, kimi/kimi-for-coding) via scripts/extract_task_features.py
label_provenance.cohort_signature: per-model pass/fail across the v0.1 comparison cohort [kimi/kimi-for-coding, minimax/minimax-m3, spark/step3p7-flash-148b, zai/glm-5.2]

labels.notes: Lazy-accrual earmark math (mulQ128). split_ifs failures: models cannot align the conditional fee branch with the conserved-total goal.
Only glm-5.2 closes it. 'Easy' label but empirically hard: the deposit-count increment requires threading the nested branch through the state update.
Divisive: minimax and glm pass, kimi and spark fail. Reasoning about pool balance across a flash-loan-via-deposit trace.
One of a family of near-identical caller-identity tasks; their FPFP/FFFP signatures are a clean model-differentiation cluster.
Bijection over sortition-tree node ids. Heavy datastructure/quantifier reasoning; at least one cohort model timed out rather than failed logically.
Solvency bound on locked funds. No cohort model solved it; failures are mostly trailing unsolved goals (close but incomplete arithmetic bound).
Refinement-equivalence is the hardest family overall (11% pass rate). Dual-asset conservation over a claim+allocate trace.
Simple storage-write postcondition that nonetheless splits the cohort; a failing attempt threw `grind` at it instead of unfolding the write.
Product-of-virtual-balances non-decreasing under swap rounding. Arithmetic-rounding monotonicity that no cohort model could discharge.
Labeled 'easy' but unsolved by the whole cohort and expensive (~650k mean tokens): a strong example of mislabeled a-priori difficulty.
theorem_missing across the board: models never produced the target declaration at all, distinct from producing a wrong proof.
Highest mean token spend of any task (~900k) with zero successes. Linked-list acyclicity over owner-manager reachability; the most expensive dead end in v0.1.
